name: KrillApp.Executor.Lambda
title: Lambda
description: Executes a Python script in a sandboxed environment when invoked. The script can read the last results of the nodes listed in the Lambda's meta.inputs, and the script's output is stored in the Lambda's own meta.snapshot; downstream observers pull it from there. Supports Firejail and Docker sandboxing with configurable memory limits (default 256MB). Scripts are stored at /opt/krill/lambdas on the server.
shortDescription: Runs a sandboxed Python script that reads its input nodes and stores its result in its own snapshot.
llmPurpose: Executes a user-defined Python script in a sandboxed environment, reading its input nodes' snapshots and storing the script result in its own meta.snapshot for observers to pull.
llmBehavior: invoked when an observed source completes (SOURCE_INVOKED) or on manual click
reads input nodes' snapshot values as script input
stores script output in its own meta.snapshot; nodes observing the Lambda are invoked
runs in sandboxed environment (Firejail or Docker)
llmOutputs: script result stored in own meta.snapshot; observers pull it from there
llmCreationHints: Create a Lambda when the user needs custom logic that cannot be expressed with built-in Calculation or LogicGate nodes. Requires a Python script file. List the DataPoints the script reads in meta.inputs and the invoking trigger/schedule in meta.sources with SOURCE_INVOKED. To persist the script's output, wire a DataPoint to observe the Lambda (its own sources and inputs).

llmConnectionHints.role: action node that executes custom Python scripts, reading its inputs and storing the result in its own snapshot
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the trigger/schedule in sources with SOURCE_INVOKED; list DataPoints the script reads in inputs; a DataPoint wanting the script's output observes this Lambda via its own sources+inputs.

llmExamples: Run a Python script to convert temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit.
Process this sensor data with a custom algorithm.
Execute a script that filters and transforms readings.
